job_description: For our DI Automation division at Siemens San. ve Tic. A.Ş. we are searching for Financial Planning & Analysis Expert who will contribute to measuring the financial performance of DI AUT while playing a key role in all our financial processes.What part will you play? Completes budget planning, financial planning, actual-versus-plan-analysis, benchmarking, forecasting and reporting activities for the management Ensures preparation of financial performance reporting for business accountable management, Supports transparency into main decision-making DI financials of P/L and asset values Analyses relevant Key Performance indicators, and provides reports, comments and recommendations, Prepares, develops and maintains controlling presentations and financial reports, Supports accountable management in planning and calculating yearly budgets, Consolidate budgets, including top-down and bottom-up plans, and proceed with revolving midterm planning, and periodic forecasts during the year, Performs comparisons actual vs.plan, and pursues effects of agreed measures, Analyses and evaluates cost structures, and builds, maintains and improves the depending on cost accounting system, Provides regular business reports, with respect to overall performance, and recommends actions to accountable management, Performs evaluation of assets and liabilities, and manages inventory measures on a regular term, Carries out ad-hoc analyses and profitability calculations, Contributes to conceptual and project work related to controlling, Manages complex big data sets and translates them into actionable insights, Executes hyperinflation reporting.What do I need to qualify for this job? Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Economics, Finance or equivalent Very good command of Turkish & English, Advanced level of knowledge on SAP, MS office programs and AI relevant programs PBI and the like, Min. 3 years of experience in fields of controlling and/or accounting Knowledge of IFRS, Turkish Tax, Turkish Commercial Code and Turkish Accounting Standards, Team analytical thinking, result and quality-oriented approach and good communication skills are required competencies.What We Offer?Speaking up CultureRespectful WorkplaceBeing part of a global work environmentAttractive remuneration packageExcellent recognition tools providing spot awardsLearning & Development opportunities for both personal and professional growth Leave days for parents and a variety of flexible working models that allow time off for yourself and your family Creche allowance for mothersShare matching programs to become a shareholder of Siemens AGRemote working and remote living flexibility only for relevant positionsFind more benefits here Individual benefits are adapted to meet local legal regulations, the requirements of different job profiles, locations, and individual preferences. At Siemens we are always challenging ourselves to build a better future. We need the most innovative and diverse Digital Minds to develop tomorrow ‘s reality.As Siemens we believe physical barriers are not related to potential. Only the potential matters to us. Therefore, we look forward to receiving applications from candidates with physical barriers and chronic illnesses. We support healthy relationships between candidates with barriers and their colleagues because we believe we can create differences together.Siemens is dedicated to quality, equality, and valuating diversity and we welcome applications that reflect the diversity of the communities within which we work.We are looking forward to receiving your online application. Please ensure you complete all areas of the application form to the best of your ability as we will use the data to review your suitability for the role.Please find more information from our web site:https://new.siemens.com/tr/tr.html Show more Show less

Where people, processes, and precision come togetherAre you detail-driven, people-oriented, and passionate about making HR operations run smoothly behind the scenes? Do you enjoy juggling payroll input, attendance data, and employee support while keeping everything compliant and on time?If that sounds like you, we’d love to meet you.As an HR Operations Officer, you’ll play a key role in supporting the day-to-day HR operations that keep our workforce moving. From attendance tracking and payroll support to onboarding, employee relations, and government documentation, you’ll be at the heart of the employee lifecycle.This role is perfect for someone who enjoys structure, accuracy, and being the go-to person for HR operational support.What You’ll Be Doing:Monitor and maintain employee attendance through the fingerprint systemPrepare and submit payroll-related documents (attendance summaries, leave records, IBANs, deductions, clearances, etc.)Track sick leaves, overtime, permissions, and compensatory offsSupport payroll validation for overtime, incentives, and allowancesHelp resolve payroll-related queries in coordination with HR and Payroll teamsAct as a first point of contact for employee HR queriesSupport employee complaint and incident documentation under supervisionCoordinate with internal departments to resolve HR and benefits-related issuesAssist with accommodation, transportation, and leave coordinationSupport onboarding documentation and system registration for new hiresPrepare employee files, contracts, and onboarding checklists.Assist with orientation sessions and HR briefings.Coordinate residency, work permits, renewals, and transfers with Government RelationsMaintain accurate records of passports, permits, and official documentsAssist with bank account setup and salary processing requirementsSupport salary distribution (cash/cheque where applicable)Ensure personnel files comply with local labor and residency lawsMaintain accurate HRIS and physical employee recordsPrepare HR reports related to attendance, leave, and payroll inputsSupport audits, data collection, and HR operational reviewsContribute to improving HR processes and system efficiencyWhat You Bring to the Table:Bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business Administration, or a related field1–3 years of experience in HR operations, administration, or HR supportExposure to payroll, personnel administration, employee relations, or HR systemsExperience with HRIS and fingerprint attendance systemsStrong Excel and Microsoft Office skillsVery good English (Arabic is a plus)Detail-oriented and organizedProfessional and confident when handling employee queriesAble to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines under pressureKnowledgeable about basic labor laws and HR operationsEager to learn, grow, and build a career in HR operationsWhy Boutiqaat?At Boutiqaat, you won’t just join a company you’ll join a movement redefining e-commerce beauty and lifestyle retail. Here, every day offers new challenges, learning opportunities, and a chance to help build a smarter, more efficient operation from the ground up.Ready to Grow with Us?Kickstart your career with hands-on experience, expert mentorship, and real impact.Apply now and be part of something exciting! job_description: Company Name: Rupayan GroupLocation: DhakaJob Type: Full-TimeWorkplace Type: Full-timeJob ID: IJOB202613477Responsibilities:Oversee the day-to-day finance and accounts operations for real estate projects and the corporate office, ensuring all financial activities align with company objectives and regulatory requirements.Maintain accurate and comprehensive books of accounts in compliance with established accounting standards and company policies, ensuring transparency and accountability in financial reporting.Prepare detailed monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, management information system (MIS) reports, and other management reports to support strategic decision-making processes.Key Duties:Handle project-wise accounting, including the allocation of land costs, construction costs, overheads, and revenue recognition, to ensure accurate financial tracking and reporting.Monitor cash flow, fund planning, and working capital requirements for ongoing and upcoming projects, ensuring financial stability and operational efficiency.Ensure timely bank reconciliation, ledger scrutiny, and control of financial transactions to maintain the integrity and accuracy of financial data.Additional Responsibilities:Collaborate with cross-functional teams to provide financial insights and support for business growth initiatives and strategic planning.Implement and maintain effective internal controls to safeguard company assets and ensure compliance with financial regulations.Lead and mentor a team of finance professionals, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development.Requirements Experience: 8-10 years Education: accounting Skills: corporate finance, project finance, back office operations, major account development, real estate development, finance management, managerial finance, senior real estate, p&lmanagement Last Date of Application: 25 February 2026 Show more Show less

KOSTAL Group is a global, family-owned German company that develops market-leading solutions for the solar and automotive industries. The company was established in 1912 and employs about 20 000 people at 47 locations in 22 countries. In Hungary, KOSTAL is building its first business service centre to provide business support in IT, finance, procurement, master data, sales and HR fields. This is a global centre with high value-added and complex processes which you can help to grow.The work can be managed remotely – with time-to-time office presence depending on the team’s agreement and the demand of the respective professional area – from any location in Hungary. In case of some company events, office attendance is expected from the colleagues. Technical conditions for working from home are fully in place.Job DescriptionOverall Purpose of the Job:The Group Internal Control Manager will play a critical role in leading and managing the internal control function within KBS. This individual will be responsible for ensuring compliance with policies and processes, as well as implementing additional controls to mitigate risks effectively. They will oversee internal control activities, provide recommendations for improvements, and enhance the organization's risk management framework. This position is crucial in ensuring that the organization's internal control framework remains robust, aligned with best practices, and supports sustainable business growth.Key Responsibilities Internal Control & Compliance Management:Develop and implement an annual risk-based Internal Control Plan to ensure compliance with internal regulations, policies, and industry standards.Conduct assessments to identify key controls within major business functions (e.g., finance, logistics, engagement, and program management).Evaluate the effectiveness of key controls through periodic testing and audits.Provide observations and recommendations for process improvements, ensuring timely follow-ups.Facilitate necessary training for staff to improve control adherence and awareness.Ensure proper documentation and monitoring of control measures to mitigate operational risks.Conduct follow-ups on recommendations from previous internal control reports.Issue an annual internal control report summarizing findings, risks, and improvements.Perform special projects such as Annual Risk Assessments and ad hoc management reports as needed. Risk Assessment & Business Process Optimization:Examine internal control systems, management operations, and accounting records for adequacy.Identify, document, and evaluate risks associated with business processes, IT systems, and operational workflows.Ensure internal control audits align with business processes and strategic objectives.Develop and maintain business process flowcharts to enhance transparency and efficiency.Assist in embedding risk awareness and control culture across the KOSTAL Group.Collaborate with operational risk teams to identify, assess, and prioritize control incidents.Provide support in implementing regulatory and risk group requirements. Leadership & Team Management:Provide guidance, mentorship, and performance input for team members.Oversee daily operations, set work priorities, and ensure smooth execution of internal control activities.Act as an advisor to senior management, briefing them on reviews, status reports, and risk mitigation strategies.Approve payments as required, ensuring adherence to control requirements.Support cross-functional collaboration to standardize best practices across the organization. HU Legal Entity Compliance:Establish an effective control environment and conduct periodic testing.Develop and enforce policies to ensure regulatory and company-wide compliance.Conduct audits to assess policy adherence and alignment with company standards.Implement quality controls over internal policies to maintain consistency and compliance. Group-Level Internal Control & Compliance:Design and implement a phased approach to internal controls over financial reporting, ensuring compliance starting from 2025.Conduct post-implementation reviews, feedback assessments, and continuous compliance monitoring.Consolidate and report on the implementation and compliance status of internal controls.Coordinate with central and regional financial reporting teams to ensure a ‘fit-for-purpose’ control environment.Align with KOSTAL Group's centralized control requirements while adapting controls to business needs.Map end-to-end key financial processes, documenting activities, risks, and associated controls.Deliver training to enhance internal controls knowledge and improve compliance and documentation quality.Perform regular monitoring activities to ensure sustained compliance.Identify control deficiencies and collaborate with Regional/Local Finance Directors to develop corrective action plans.Develop an internal assurance/attestation plan for continuous monitoring and compliance.Work with Digital Transform

About TideAt Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding, low fees and innovative features, we thrive on making data-driven decisions to serve our mission: to help SMEs save both time (and money) so they can get back to doing what they love.Tide FactsTide is available for UK, Indian, German and French SMEsOver 1.8 million members: 800,000 UK and 1,000,000 in India and growing rapidlyOver $300 million raised in fundingOver 2,500 Tideans globally - we’re diversity champions!We have offices in Central London, with a member support and technology centre in Sofia, Bulgaria, technology centres in Serbia, Romania, Lithuania and Hyderabad and offices in Gurugram and New Delhi, and in Berlin, Paris and Luxembourg.About The TeamThe Enforcement team plays a vital part in tackling fraud and financial crime with a strong emphasis on disrupting perpetrators via external reports to law enforcement agencies and handling inbound requests from the authorities.About The RoleThis role is a key element of our Fincrime Intelligence and Investigative Reporting (FIR) Team. You’ll be responsible for ensuring we put our genuine members first, through reporting potentially suspicious activity to authorities and taking measures to disrupt criminals. You’ll have opportunities to be involved in wider Financial Crime projects which will help reduce risk to Tide and let our members get back to doing what they love.As An Enforcement And Reporting Analyst You WillCollate information relating to all types of financial crime for inclusion within reports to Law Enforcement Agencies, including SAR submissionsUse various enforcement techniques to disrupt bad actors who try to use Tide or target our members. Be confident in calling out potential efficiencies in our tools and processes used for Enforcement and ReportingManage your own case load to meet performance targetsConsistently reach the targets for Productivity and Quality Make recommendations for process improvements to prevent future occurrences of fraud/financial crime offencesEstablish good relationships with various departments throughout the organisation to ensure good operational synergyParticipate in internal and external training programs related to fraud/financial crime offences detection and prevention and other subjects that may form part of the day-to-day work requirementsTreat Tide members fairly by taking responsibility to ensure that their needs are metWhat We Are Looking ForHere at Tide, we are looking for a proactively minded Enforcement and Reporting Analyst with a good understanding of Fraud and Financial Crime typologies and prevention measures. We need a passionate and proactive individual, who will help ensure Tide is taking all the relevant steps to report and enforce against all aspects of financial crime.The Ideal Candidate Will Have1 to 3 years of experience in a financial crime related role in financial services, specialising in business-related anti-money laundering/fraud investigations Ability to work well as part of a team; decisive; goal-oriented; proactiveStrong stakeholder management skills and comfortable communicating with a wide range of associatesA proactive and open-minded approach to finding effective solutionsA genuinely curious mindset, asking the right questions at the right times, with a problem solving attitudeA keen eye for process improvements, looking at how we can work leaner and more robustlyExceptional communication skills – written and verbal – and are able to communicate with our members, internal stakeholders & regulatory bodies if requiredWhat You’ll Get In Return25 days paid annual leave 3 paid days off for volunteering or L&D activitiesExtended maternity and paternity leave covered by the companyPersonal L&D budget in the amount of 1000 BGN per yearAdditional health & dental insuranceMental wellbeing platform Fully covered Multisports cardFood vouchersSnacks, light food, drinks in the officeWFH equipment allowanceFlexible working from homeSabbatical LeaveTransportation bonus to cover office commute under the hybrid work arrangementTIDEAN WAYS OF WORKINGAt Tide, we champion a flexible workplace model that supports both in-person and remote work to cater to the specific needs of our different teams.While remote work is supported, we believe in the power of face-to-face interactions to foster team spirit and collaboration. Our offices are designed as hubs for innovation and team-building, where we encourage regular in-person gatherings to foster a strong sense of community.TIDE IS A PLACE FOR EVERYONEA

fichiers numériques (SharePoint/OneDrive) et mettre à jour procédures et registres;Mettre en place un tableau de bord et priorités;Faciliter l’intégration des nouveaux employés et soutenir les équipes dans leurs besoins quotidiens;Assurer la qualité des livrables;Optimiser les tâches répétitives avec des outils numériques et une automatisation simple (Power Automate, Forms).🎯Profil idéalDEC en français à l’oral et à l’écrit.💛Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?Jouez un rôle clé au cœur de l’équipe et ayez un impact direct sur l'efficacité;Développez vos compétences numériques et organisationnelles;Travaillez dans un environnement dynamique, collaboratif et stimulant;Contribuez à l’amélioration continue et à l’innovation dans les processus administratifs;Travail hybride et horaires flexibles : Horaire de 35 heures par semaine, politique de travail 100% flexible, vous offrant la liberté de choisir votre mode et votre environnement de travail;Congé les vendredis après-midi;Espaces de travail modernes et accueillants : Si vous choisissez de travailler au bureau, vous aurez l'occasion de découvrir nos tout nouveaux espaces de travail, entièrement rénovés et conçus pour vous fournir un milieu de vie professionnel stimulant et accueillant.Notez que des tests de compétences feront partie du processus.Au plaisir de discuter avec vous !

Group Financial ControllerMy client is a large group with operations across Europe, US & Asia. Set up and embed a culture of continuous improvement delivering optimum performance.Prepare accurate and timely financial management reports within agreed timelines to the senior leadership team and boardPrepare and submit all necessary statutory reporting (audits, tax returns, etc.) required in Ireland, the USA, and other relevant markets, consistent with international accounting procedures and regulatory compliance protocolsSupport the CFO in delivering key strategic finance objectives, including mergers and acquisitions, refinancing, capital expenditures, and investor relationsImplement relevant technology platforms and data analytic tools to optimise financial processes and reportingEffectively manage key internal (senior leadership team and board) and external (auditors, regulatory bodies, and banks) stakeholder relationshipsThe person:Qualified Accountant5+ years of finance experience with a strong background in accounting and financial controlStrong understanding of financial reporting standards (IFRS and GAAP)Advanced Microsoft Excel and financial modelling skillsKnowledge of ERP and BI systemsExperience in multinational organisation requiredSalary: €100k - €110k plus benefitsHybrid working, 3 days on site.

job_title: Senior Consultant / Manager SAP Finance - Controlling CO (w/m/d)
job_description: Mit Prozessen in der DNA zum Erfolg. Prozesse sind die Basis unseres Handelns. Der Erfolg jedes Unternehmens hängt von ihrer Qualität ab. Scheer steht für Qualität. Wir sind neugierig auf innovative Technologien und wollen diese intelligent und verantwortungsvoll, mit Leidenschaft und Freude bei unseren Kunden umsetzen.Der Scheer Spirit. In unserer offenen Unternehmenskultur leben wir ein partnerschaftliches und wertschätzendes Miteinander sowie eine Kommunikation auf Augenhöhe. Die Scheer Gruppe zeichnet sich aus, durch einen starken Zusammenhalt der Teams. Du kannst individuell für Dich entscheiden, wie Dein Weg bei Scheer aussehen soll und wir unterstützen Dich dabei.Unsere Expert*innen im Bereich SAP Business Consulting überzeugen durch ihre branchenübergreifende Erfahrung und Innovationskraft bei der Optimierung von Geschäftsprozessen. job_description: Senior Indirect Tax Analyst - North York, ON (Retail Industry)Compensation: $80,000-$100,000 + total package About The Role Hays have partnered with a Canadian retail organization who are seeking a highly analytical, detail‑oriented individual who thrives in a dynamic environment and can work collaboratively with cross‑functional teams. Reporting to the Sr. Tax Manager, the new Indirect Tax Analyst role will support the end‑to‑end indirect tax function, focusing on timely compliance, effective audit management, process improvement, and operational support for indirect tax systems and data. Key Responsibilities Indirect Tax Compliance, Process & Risk ManagementManage the Indirect tax calendar to ensure timely filings, and co-ordinate any necessary GST, HST QST and PST remittances.Reconciliation of GST/HST/QST/PST statements and notices to accounting records, independently resolve discrepancies with the business and tax authorities.Review current processes and procedures, identify process inefficiencies and suggest opportunities for workflow improvements. Audit SupportCoordinate and prepare responses to indirect tax audit queries, including information gathering, data extraction, and documentation support for GST/HST, PST/QST reviewsAssist with reviewing audit assessments, understanding underlying issues, and helping drive corrective actions.Maintain organized audit files and contribute to continuous audit‑readiness improvements. Tax Technology & Data AnalysisWork with the business and IT to ensure accurate tax application and execute other tax related system updates and process improvements.Support digital transformation initiatives, including testing tax‑sensitive system changes and validating expected outcomes.Build and maintain working datasets to support compliance, audit, and internal reporting. Special ProjectsIndependently research, analyze and document indirect tax issues or legislative changes.Support execution of tax projects and initiatives as assigned. Minimum Requirements Accounting designation (CPA) complete or actively working towards attaining, is preferred.Bachelor’s degree in accounting, tax, math, data science or related field.Minimum 1 to 3 years in tax, accounting or data analytics,Experience with indirect tax compliance or audit support is an asset.Proficiency in ERP system (e.g. SAP), and familiarity with tax software platforms (e.g. SOVOS, Vertex)Retail industry experience a plus. Skills And Attributes for SuccessTechnical abilities like GBQ, SQL and programing languages like Python along with data visualization and statistical knowledge.Strong interpersonal, analytical, and problem-solving skills, and the ability to solve complex problems using both accounting principles and mathematical techniques.Proven ability to manage time efficiently and stay organized in a dynamic work environment.Eagerness to learn and adapt within a tax technology landscape.Capacity to excel in a fast-paced professional services setting, demonstrating resilience and agility.A proactive, team-oriented mindset with a high level of motivation and commitment to achieving results

job_description: Job DescriptionChange the world. Love your job.In your first year with TI, you will participate in the Career Accelerator Program (CAP), which provides professional and technical training and resources to accelerate your ramp into TI, and set you up for long-term career success. Within this program, we also offer function-specific technical training and on-the-job learning opportunities that will encourage you to solve problems through a variety of hands-on, meaningful experiences from your very first day on the job.In a Financial Analyst role you will provide leadership in the areas of financial forecasting, efficiency improvements, reporting enhancements, risk and profitability analyses, and financial modeling. Your role may be in one or more of these areas: Manufacturing, Business Unit, Financial PlanningResponsibilities IncludeDriving financial excellence and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of the financial results for the businessCross functional partnering to ensure financial complianceUnderstanding and explaining the relationship between operational performance, financial results and complianceProviding a strategic view of the business and processes by incorporating competitive analysis to drive financial performanceContinuous process improvement and automation in financial processes and reporting activitiesPut your talent to work with Texas Instruments!QualificationsMinimum requirements:Bachelor's degree in finance or related business degreeCumulative 3.0/4.0 GPA or higher Fresh graduates or graduates with less than 2 years working experience Preferred QualificationsStrong understanding of internal control principles and accounting policies and proceduresSystems knowledge/skills including: Excel, SAPStrong analytical and problem-solving skillsStrong written and verbal communication skillsAbility to team and collaborate effectively with people in different functions, countries and culturesAbility to build strong, influential relationshipsAbility to take initiative, prioritize work, balance multiple projects and drive for results in a fast-paced and rapidly changing environmentNavigates ambiguityAttention to detailAbout UsWhy TI?Engineer your future. We empower our employees to truly own their career and development. Come collaborate with some of the smartest people in the world to shape the future of electronics.We're different by design. Diverse backgrounds and perspectives are what push innovation forward and what make TI stronger. We value each and every voice, and look forward to hearing yours. Meet the people of TI Benefits that benefit you. We offer competitive pay and benefits designed to help you and your family live your best life. Your well-being is important to us.About Texas InstrumentsTexas Instruments Incorporated (Nasdaq: TXN) is a global semiconductor company that designs, manufactures and sells analog and embedded processing chips for markets such as industrial, automotive, data center, personal electronics and communications equipment. At our core, we have a passion to create a better world by making electronics more affordable through semiconductors. This passion is alive today as each generation of innovation builds upon the last to make our technology more reliable, more affordable and lower power, making it possible for semiconductors to go into electronics everywhere. Learn more at TI.com .Texas Instruments is an equal opportunity employer and supports a diverse, inclusive work environment.If you are interested in this position, please apply to this requisition.

PRINTAdministrative and Financial Assistant27.02.2026 Expiring soonTextImageCopyrightEuropean UnionVACANCY ANNOUNCEMENT FOR THE POST OFADMINISTRATIVE AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANT (Group III)ECHO COX’S BAZAR SUB COUNTRY OFFICEThe European Commission’s Directorate-General for European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations (DG ECHO) funds relief operations for victims of natural disasters and conflicts outside the European Union. For humanitarian aid, the Commission works with about 200 operational partners, including United Nations agencies, the Red Cross/Crescent movement, and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). Aid is channelled impartially, straight to victims, regardless of their race, ethnic group, religion, gender, age, nationality, or political affiliation.ECHO announces the position for Administrative and Financial Assistant for its office based in Cox’s Bazar. This position is open to nationals and other residents of Bangladesh with valid work and residence permits.DG ECHO applies a policy of equal opportunities. Our recruitment policy is based on respect of diversity, maintaining the gender balance, as well as the balance between professional and private life, and support for training and developments opportunities.We welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons; female candidates are encouraged to apply.Job DescriptionThe Administrative and Financial Assistant plans and executes all necessary office administrative and financial functions, and carries out finance, accounting and administration duties, crisis support on finance and human resources and other related duties. The Administrative and Financial Assistant commits to strict confidentiality.EducationJOB REQUIREMENTSA relevant first level university degree or equivalent professional experience.Knowledge And ExperienceMinimum 5 years of relevant work experience at national or international level in administration or programme support service, including experience in procurement, human resources and finances.Previous experience in a Governmental or other International Organisation is an advantage.Very Good drafting skills.Good communication skills.Ability to work under pressure in a multitasking position.Ability to work in a multicultural environment.Experience in the usage of computers and office software packages (MS Word, Excel, etc.).LanguagesFluency in English (oral and written) An excellent knowledge of Bangla (oral and written)How To ApplyApplications MUST comprise of:A cover letter (maximum of 1 page) setting out the candidate’s motivation and suitability for the position;A detailed CV in the European Commission (EC) standard format only. The EC Standard format can be downloaded from this link. No other format will be accepted.Academic and relevant employment certificates certified true copy by the applicant;The name, position and contact number/details (telephone, e-mail) of three references (including HR department and the supervisor), one of them being a recent employer;A copy of the valid work and residence permit to live and work in Bangladesh (in case of non-Bangladeshi nationality)Applications should be uploaded at https://ats.flexi-personnel.com until 15/03/2026 at 17:00hrs (local time). The vacancy reference REF: “ECHO/CXB/AFA2026” must be clearly indicated in the subject line.Only the short-listed candidates will be contacted. Candidates MUST abstain from contacting the ECHO Cox’s Bazar office for information on the selection process or for any other purpose. Any form of canvassing, soliciting, or influencing will be treated as grounds for disqualification.Note: Flexi Personnel Limited has been contracted by ECHO to handle the recruitment process of this position.THE EMPLOYMENT CONDITIONSThe contract is initially for one year with possibility of renewal subject to a successful completion a 3- month probationary period and following a positive annual evaluation.As an indication, the monthly basic salary range is on average approximately BDT 135,647 which corresponds to five years of relevant experience and will be adapted according to the exact duration of relevant professional experience supported by a work certificate. Besides the basic salary, the employee will receive disability/retirement allowance, 13th month salary, transportation allowance, as well as a medical coverage. (The salary is subject to local taxes.)ECHO employees observe the National Staff code of Conduct which is available on the link:https://civil-protection-humanitarian-aid.ec.europa.eu/who/about-echo/field-network_en“Any processing of your personal data will be done in accordance with the applicable EU legislation (Regulation (EU) 2018/1725 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 October 2018 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data by the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies and on the free movement of such data.”The privacy statement can be found on ECHO website.https

job_description: Our client is a Tax Consulting firm seeking a Senior Tax Manager to lead tax engagements and manage relationships with key stakeholders.The ideal candidate will serve as a primary point of contact for clients, overseeing the execution of tax transactions while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. The role will involve identifying business opportunities, leading a team of tax professionals, and contributing to the firm’s strategic goals.Type: Full-timeKey Responsibilities Tax Strategy and Planning: Develop and implement comprehensive tax strategies for clients in line with local and international tax regulations. Provide strategic tax planning for businesses and individuals to optimize tax obligations, ensuring full compliance with Nigerian tax laws and international tax frameworks. Tax Compliance: Oversee the preparation and timely submission of tax returns (corporate income tax, VAT, withholding tax, PAYE, etc.) for clients. Ensure accurate and up-to-date maintenance of tax records, including audits, assessments, and statutory filings. Stay abreast of changes in Nigerian tax legislation, policies, and procedures, and communicate the impact of these changes to clients and internal teams. Client Advisory and Representation: Provide advisory services to clients on tax-efficient business structuring, mergers and acquisitions, cross-border transactions, and other complex tax matters. Represent clients in discussions and negotiations with tax authorities (FIRS, state tax authorities), including dispute resolution, tax audits, and investigations. Team Leadership and Development: Lead, mentor, and develop junior tax professionals within the consulting team, ensuring the continuous development of technical skills and industry knowledge. Review and approve tax work prepared by junior staff, providing constructive feedback to enhance quality and accuracy. Coordinate and collaborate with other departments such as legal and finance to provide holistic solutions to clients. Risk Management and Compliance Review: Conduct risk assessments to identify tax exposures and ensure that all tax advisory and compliance activities mitigate these risks. Implement tax risk management policies and procedures, ensuring clients comply with Nigerian tax regulations and international best practices. Monitor tax law changes and advise on tax risk mitigation strategies. Business Development: Identify new business opportunities, develop client relationships, and participate in business development activities. Develop proposals, presentations, and bids for new projects, tailoring solutions to meet client-specific tax needs. Build strong, long-term relationships with clients and act as their primary contact for all tax-related matters. Specialized Tax Advisory: Provide expert advice on specialized areas of taxation such as transfer pricing, international tax, indirect tax (VAT), and customs duties. Support clients with tax planning in relation to investments, real estate, financial products, and emerging sectors such as the digital economy and oil and gas.EducationQualifications and Requirements:A professional certification in taxation (e.g., CITN, ACCA) is required.Experience Minimum of 6-8 years of progressive experience in tax advisory and compliance, preferably with a tax consulting firm and with at least 2 years in a tax managerial role. Proven track record in managing complex tax projects and handling tax audits, disputes, and negotiations with tax authorities. Technical Expertise: Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax regulations, including CIT, VAT, transfer pricing, and withholding tax. Experience with international tax law, double taxation treaties, and cross-border transactions.Familiarity with tax planning tools and ERP systems such as SAP or Oracle. Leadership and Management:Proven leadership skills with experience in managing teams and delivering high-quality client services.Excellent organizational and project management skills, with the ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines. Problem Solving and Analytical Skills:Strong analytical, research, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to navigate complex tax issues and provide innovative solutions.Ability to interpret and apply tax laws in practical scenarios, demonstrating keen attention to detail. Communication and Interpersonal Skills:Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain complex tax matters in a clear and understandable way.Ability to build and maintain strong relationships with clients, tax authorities, and stakeholders. Ethical Standards:High level of integrity, professionalism, and adherence to ethical practices in tax consulting.‍‍ Show more Show less

Join Our Team as an Accountant (XERO) at HBD Services! Company: HBD Services.Position: Accountant (XERO)Vacancy: 2Location: RemoteEmployment: Full-time About UsHBD Services is a leading study abroad agency committed to empowering students with the knowledge and resources they need to pursue international education. We take pride in our dedicated team that plays a crucial role in making students' dreams of studying abroad a reality. Job DescriptionWe are looking for a detail-oriented and experienced Accountant to manage our financial transactions and ensure the financial health of our organization. The accountant will play a key role in maintaining accurate financial records, preparing financial reports, and contributing to the overall financial stability of HBD Services. Key Responsibilities· Maintain and update financial records, including accounts payable and accounts receivable.· Prepare, examine, and analyze financial statements and reports to ensure accuracy and compliance with accounting principles and regulations.· Handle day-to-day financial transactions, including invoicing, receipts, and disbursements.· Reconcile financial discrepancies by collecting and analyzing account information.· Assist in the preparation of budgets and financial forecasts.· Monitor and track expenses, ensuring cost-effectiveness and compliance with budgetary constraints.· Prepare and submit tax returns and other regulatory filings.· Provide financial analysis and recommendations to support business decision-making.· Collaborate with the finance team to develop and implement financial policies and procedures.· Assist in the annual financial audit process and liaise with auditors.· Stay up-to-date with changes in financial regulations and best practices.· Accounts Payables and Receivables in XERO.· Bank Reconciliation in XERO.· Import all pending invoices and bills from XERO to create a breakdown of the invoices and statements.· Input all invoice and bill details into Zapier to import them into XERO.· Respond to emails and handle other necessary communications.· Clear outstanding payment folders.· Contact the Head of Operations for pricing.· Create bank payments.· Generate invoices for clients, including pricing and costs, etc.· Update the RTOs scope list and pricing, etc.· Dispatch scanned copies of certificates to agents and clients. Requirements· Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.· Proven work experience as an Accountant, with a minimum of 2 years in a similar role.· Solid knowledge of accounting principles, practices, and regulations.· Must have worked in XERO or similar software.· Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel.· Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.· Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.· Ability to work independently and as part of a team.· Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.· Knowledge of international financial reporting standards is a plus. What We Offer:· Competitive salary based on your experience and expertise.· Opportunities for professional development and growth.· A supportive and collaborative work environment.· The chance to contribute to an organization that helps students achieve their educational dreams.[Additional benefits as per company policy.] Apply Now:If you are a dedicated and meticulous Accountant looking to be part of a dynamic team that values financial integrity and accountability, we invite you to apply. Join HBD Services in making a positive impact on students' lives. Send your CV to admin@hbdservices.com and help us manage our financial success!

Celebrating 54 years of growth, Sym-Tech is a leading finance and insurance provider to the retail automotive industry. At-Sym-Tech we are looking for Associates who share our passion for innovation, creativity and results! We are passionate about delivering excellent client service, and an outstanding Associate experience with opportunities to learn and grow professionally. Sym-Tech is growing quickly and that means endless ways to make a real difference! We are currently recruiting an Intermediate Accountant, to join our headquarters located in Thornhill, ON.A career with us offers:A fun, fast paced cultureOpportunities to grow and develop your careerOn-going industry training programs and professional development opportunitiesA workplace that supports workplace diversity, equity and inclusionA strong promoter of women in the automotive industryThe chance to work with some of the best in the business!Position Summary:Working closely with the senior Financial Leaders (Director of Finance, Controller and Corporate team), the Intermediate Accountant will provide support to the team with respect to financial reporting analysis and special projects. This individual will assist with the month-end close, perform financial analyses to understand results and assist with decision making, and assist with the annual budget process. This role incorporates a combination of financial reporting, financial analysis, and special projects to ensure that corporate business objectives and financial results are met.Position Functions:Prepare accurate and timely financial reporting for the Company.Reconcile and analyze monthly balance sheet, income statements, financial position, cash flow forecasts and budgets.Maintain a and trends; effectively communicate key business insights to management and business partners.Assist with the corporate budgeting process including updating forecasts and maintaining projection models.Review actual versus forecast and communicate with operational business leaders to provide financial insight into performance and variance analysis.Assist with external auditors and prepare reports for financial institutions, as required, including overseeing any auditing conducted by third parties to ensure effective resolution and swift closure of auditing activities.Enhance the accuracy of the company’s reported financial results and ensure that reported results comply with GAAP.Train and develop a team of junior staff within the accounting team.Completes project work as required and participates in all reasonable work activities as may be deemed suitable and assigned by management.Minimum Qualifications & Competencies:University degree in Business/Accounting, with conflicting priorities.Strong preference for candidates who have worked in an entrepreneurial, high growth environment in a privately held business.Demonstrated ability to “roll up the sleeves” and
